english: (_jig-rten-skyóṅ ('jig rten skyong) v. skyoṅ-ba (skyong ba)), residing just below the summit of Meru, the protectors of the gods against the Asuras, v. Köpp. I, 250; II, 261. — rgyal-stód (rgyal stod) lunar mansion v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar). — rgyál-po (rgyal po) 1. king, rgyál-po čéṅ-po (rgyal po chen po) great king, emperor; rgyál-por _júg-pa (rgyal por 'jug pa), bskó-ba (bsko ba), to inaugurate a king, to raise to the royal throne; mí-la rgyál-po _čól-ba (mi la rgyal po 'chol ba) id. Pth.; rgyál-po byéd-pa (rgyal po byed pa) to act the king, to be(a) king; ṅa rgyál-po mi _dod (nga rgyal po mi 'dod) I do not wish to be king Dzl.; rgyál-po mi tób-na (rgyal po mi thob na) if I do not obtain royalty Dzl.; ṅas ni rgyál-po mi nús-so (ngas ni rgyal po mi nus so) I cannot be king Dzl. The word is also used for: government-authorities, police etc.; rgyál-poi čád-pa (rgyal po'i chad pa) public punishment, rgyál-pos gsód-pa (rgyal pos gsod pa) to be publicly executed. (As a characteristic sign of Asiatic views it seems worth mentioning, that the rgyal-po (rgyal po) is usually spoken of much in the same manner, as robbers, conflagrations etc. are, i.e. as a kind of calamity against which protection is to be sought, esp. by charms and spells, cf. _jigs-pa ('jigs pa)). 2. a local god, _gro-táṅ rgyál-po ('gro thang rgyal po) the Dewa of Dotaṅ Mil. 3. fig. something excellent, superior in its kind; not only as with us the word is applied to the lion, as the king of animals, but also to distinguished flowers: the Udumbara (Ficus glomerata), to mountains, Meru and others; and col. gaṅs-rgyál (gangs rgyal) a large glacier, brag-rgyál (brag rgyal) a huge rock, smóṅ-lam-gyi rgyál-po (smon lam gyi rgyal po) a very comprehensive prayer, the bzaṅ-spyód (bzang spyod) Glr. 4. symb. num.: 16. — rgyal-pʻrán (rgyal phran) vassal or feudatory prince. — rgyál-bu (rgyal bu) prince. — rgyal-blóṅ (rgyal blon) king and ministers, council of state. — rgyál-mo (rgyal mo) 1. queen. 2. pupil of the eye, together with the iris; rgyál-moi mdaṅs nyams (rgyal mo'i mdangs nyams) the brightness of the eyeball disappears Med. 3. like rgyál-po (rgyal po) 3, e.g. a charm of particular power. — rgyal-smád (rgyal smad) lunar mansion, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar). — rgyal-tsáb (rgyal tshab) (for rgyal-poi tsab (rgyal po'i tshab)) 1. vice-roy, regent. Such a vice-roy under Chinese supremacy is now the king of Tibet, who about a century ago was still an independent ruler. 2. successor of a king. 3. (for rgyál-bai tsab (rgyal ba'i tshab)) Maitreya, the future Buddha. — rgyal-mtsán (rgyal mtshan) sign of victory, trophy, a kind of decoration of cloth, of a cylindrical shape, erected upon a flag-staff, or carried on a pole. — rgyal-rábs (rgyal rabs) 1. = rgyal-brgyúd (rgyal brgyud). 2. history, annals, of the kings, title of several books. — rgyal-rígs (rgyal rigs) 1. the royal family, house, lineage, 2. warrior-caste क्षत्र. — rgyál-sa (rgyal sa) 1. a king's or prince's residence, city where a court is held, and hence often capital, metropolis. 2. col., esp. in W.: town. 3. throne fig.; rgyál-sar pʻyégs-pa (rgyal sar phyegs pa) to ascend the throne, rgyal-sa bzúṅ-ba (rgyal sa bzung ba) to occupy the throne, rgyál-sar bskó-ba (rgyal sar bsko ba) to raise to the throne, rgyal-sa _pʻrog-pa (rgyal sa 'phrog pa) to usurp the throne; _di-nas rgyál-sa rgyai miṅ rgyál-po-la śor ('di nas rgyal sa rgya'i ming rgyal po la shor) from him the dominion passed over to the Chinese Ming-dynasty Glr. — rgyal-srás (rgyal sras) 1. prince. 2. son of Buddha, a saint; sṅón-gyi rgyal-srás (sngon gyi rgyal sras) saints of the olden time, of past ages. — rgyal-sríd (rgyal srid) 1. government, reign, rgyal-sríd bzúṅ-ba (rgyal srid bzung ba) to enter upon the reign, to take possession of the throne. 2. rgyal-sríd sna-bdún (rgyal srid sna bdun) the seven jewels of royal government, v. rin-čéṅ (rin chen).

(prop. pf. to rgyá-ba (rgya ba)) 1. vb. to increase in bulk or quantity, to augment, to spread, bá-yi nu ltar rgyas (ba yi nu ltar rgyas) the swollen uvula gets as big as a cow's dug (these are in Tibet particularly small) Lt.; _ja-tsón rgyás-pa _dra ('ja' tshon rgyas pa 'dra) like an expanded rainbow Glr.; bstán-pa rgyás-śiṅ (bstan pa rgyas shing) the doctrine gaining ground, spreading Glr.; to grow, develop itself, of blossoms frq., of the body etc. — 2. adj. extensive, large, ample, wide; copious, plentiful, manifold, numerous; rich in, abounding in; great in, strong in cca.; detailed, complete, full; esp. adv. rgyás-par (rgyas par) (col. *gyás-pa (gyas pa), gye-pa (gye pa)*), rgyás-par śes _dód-na (rgyas par shes 'dod na), often also rgyás-par _dód-na (rgyas par 'dod na) if you wish to know it fully, to hear it in detail; *tsáṅ-ma gyás-pa zér-na* (tshang ma gyas pa zer na) W. if all the particulars are to be told; rgyás-par byéd-pa (rgyas par byed pa) 1. to make bigger, to augment, to increase, to bestow or confer plentifully, mi-la (mi la) on a person Glr. 2. to describe, narrate, state at large, in detail frq.; don rgyás-par byed-pa (don rgyas par byed pa) to be very useful, to exert a
